speaker: PRINCESS
text: Sweethearts, we shall be rich ere we depart,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: If fairings come thus plentifully in.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: A lady walled about with diamonds!

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Look you what I have from the loving king.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Madam, came nothing else along with that?

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Nothing but this? Yes, as much love in rhyme

speaker: PRINCESS
text: As would be crammed up in a sheet of paper

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Writ o’ both sides the leaf, margent and all,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: That he was fain to seal on Cupid’s name.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: That was the way to make his godhead wax,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: For he hath been five thousand year a boy.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Ay, and a shrewd unhappy gallows, too.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: You’ll ne’er be friends with him. He killed your sister.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: He made her melancholy, sad, and heavy,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And so she died. Had she been light like you,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Of such a merry, nimble, stirring spirit,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: She might ha’ been a grandam ere she died.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And so may you, for a light heart lives long.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: What’s your dark meaning, mouse, of this light word?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: A light condition in a beauty dark.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: We need more light to find your meaning out.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: You’ll mar the light by taking it in snuff;

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Therefore I’ll darkly end the argument.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Look what you do, you do it still i’ th’ dark.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: So do not you, for you are a light wench.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Indeed, I weigh not you, and therefore light.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: You weigh me not? O, that’s you care not for me.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Great reason: for past care is still past cure.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Well bandied both; a set of wit well played.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: But, Rosaline, you have a favor too.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Who sent it? And what is it?

speaker: ROSALINE
text: I would you knew.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: An if my face were but as fair as yours,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: My favor were as great. Be witness this.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Nay, I have verses too, I thank Berowne;

speaker: ROSALINE
text: The numbers true; and were the numb’ring too,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: I were the fairest goddess on the ground.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: I am compared to twenty thousand fairs.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: O, he hath drawn my picture in his letter.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Anything like?

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Much in the letters, nothing in the praise.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Beauteous as ink: a good conclusion.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Fair as a text B in a copybook.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Ware pencils, ho! Let me not die your debtor,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: My red dominical, my golden letter.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: O, that your face were not so full of O’s!

speaker: PRINCESS
text: A pox of that jest! And I beshrew all shrows.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: But, Katherine, what was sent to you

speaker: PRINCESS
text: From fair Dumaine?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Madam, this glove.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Did he not send you twain?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Yes, madam, and moreover,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Some thousand verses of a faithful lover,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: A huge translation of hypocrisy,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Vilely compiled, profound simplicity.

speaker: MARIA
text: This, and these pearls, to me sent Longaville.

speaker: MARIA
text: The letter is too long by half a mile.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: I think no less. Dost thou not wish in heart

speaker: PRINCESS
text: The chain were longer and the letter short?

speaker: MARIA
text: Ay, or I would these hands might never part.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: We are wise girls to mock our lovers so.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: They are worse fools to purchase mocking so.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: That same Berowne I’ll torture ere I go.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: O, that I knew he were but in by th’ week,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: How I would make him fawn, and beg, and seek,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: And wait the season, and observe the times,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: And spend his prodigal wits in bootless rhymes,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: And shape his service wholly to my hests,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: And make him proud to make me proud that jests!

speaker: ROSALINE
text: So pair-taunt-like would I o’ersway his state,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: That he should be my fool, and I his fate.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: None are so surely caught, when they are catched,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: As wit turned fool. Folly in wisdom hatched

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Hath wisdom’s warrant and the help of school,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: And wit’s own grace to grace a learnèd fool.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: The blood of youth burns not with such excess

speaker: ROSALINE
text: As gravity’s revolt to wantonness.

speaker: MARIA
text: Folly in fools bears not so strong a note

speaker: MARIA
text: As fool’ry in the wise, when wit doth dote,

speaker: MARIA
text: Since all the power thereof it doth apply

speaker: MARIA
text: To prove, by wit, worth in simplicity.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Here comes Boyet, and mirth is in his face.

speaker: BOYET
text: O, I am stabbed with laughter. Where’s her Grace?

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Thy news, Boyet?

speaker: BOYET
text: Prepare, madam, prepare.

speaker: BOYET
text: Arm, wenches, arm. Encounters mounted are

speaker: BOYET
text: Against your peace. Love doth approach, disguised,

speaker: BOYET
text: Armèd in arguments. You’ll be surprised.

speaker: BOYET
text: Muster your wits, stand in your own defense,

speaker: BOYET
text: Or hide your heads like cowards, and fly hence.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Saint Denis to Saint Cupid! What are they

speaker: PRINCESS
text: That charge their breath against us? Say, scout, say.

speaker: BOYET
text: Under the cool shade of a sycamore,

speaker: BOYET
text: I thought to close mine eyes some half an hour.

speaker: BOYET
text: When, lo, to interrupt my purposed rest,

speaker: BOYET
text: Toward that shade I might behold addressed

speaker: BOYET
text: The King and his companions. Warily

speaker: BOYET
text: I stole into a neighbor thicket by,

speaker: BOYET
text: And overheard what you shall overhear:

speaker: BOYET
text: That, by and by, disguised, they will be here.

speaker: BOYET
text: Their herald is a pretty knavish page

speaker: BOYET
text: That well by heart hath conned his embassage.

speaker: BOYET
text: Action and accent did they teach him there:

speaker: BOYET
text: “Thus must thou speak,” and “thus thy body bear.”

speaker: BOYET
text: And ever and anon they made a doubt

speaker: BOYET
text: Presence majestical would put him out;

speaker: BOYET
text: “For,” quoth the King, “an angel shalt thou see;

speaker: BOYET
text: Yet fear not thou, but speak audaciously.”

speaker: BOYET
text: The boy replied “An angel is not evil.

speaker: BOYET
text: I should have feared her had she been a devil.”

speaker: BOYET
text: With that, all laughed and clapped him on the shoulder,

speaker: BOYET
text: Making the bold wag by their praises bolder.

speaker: BOYET
text: One rubbed his elbow thus, and fleered, and swore

speaker: BOYET
text: A better speech was never spoke before.

speaker: BOYET
text: Another with his finger and his thumb,

speaker: BOYET
text: Cried “Via! We will do ’t, come what will come.”

speaker: BOYET
text: The third he capered and cried “All goes well!”

speaker: BOYET
text: The fourth turned on the toe, and down he fell.

speaker: BOYET
text: With that, they all did tumble on the ground

speaker: BOYET
text: With such a zealous laughter so profound

speaker: BOYET
text: That in this spleen ridiculous appears,

speaker: BOYET
text: To check their folly, passion’s solemn tears.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: But what, but what? Come they to visit us?

speaker: BOYET
text: They do, they do; and are appareled thus,

speaker: BOYET
text: Like Muscovites, or Russians, as I guess.

speaker: BOYET
text: Their purpose is to parley, to court, and dance,

speaker: BOYET
text: And every one his love-feat will advance

speaker: BOYET
text: Unto his several mistress—which they’ll know

speaker: BOYET
text: By favors several which they did bestow.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: And will they so? The gallants shall be tasked,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: For, ladies, we will every one be masked,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: And not a man of them shall have the grace,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Despite of suit, to see a lady’s face.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Hold, Rosaline, this favor thou shalt wear,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: And then the King will court thee for his dear.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Hold, take thou this, my sweet, and give me thine.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: So shall Berowne take me for Rosaline.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: And change you favors too. So shall your loves

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Woo contrary, deceived by these removes.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Come on, then, wear the favors most in sight.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: But in this changing, what is your intent?

speaker: PRINCESS
text: The effect of my intent is to cross theirs.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: They do it but in mockery merriment,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: And mock for mock is only my intent.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Their several counsels they unbosom shall

speaker: PRINCESS
text: To loves mistook, and so be mocked withal

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Upon the next occasion that we meet,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: With visages displayed, to talk and greet.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: But shall we dance, if they desire us to ’t?

speaker: PRINCESS
text: No, to the death we will not move a foot,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Nor to their penned speech render we no grace,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: But while ’tis spoke each turn away her face.

speaker: BOYET
text: Why, that contempt will kill the speaker’s heart,

speaker: BOYET
text: And quite divorce his memory from his part.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Therefore I do it, and I make no doubt

speaker: PRINCESS
text: The rest will ne’er come in if he be out.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: There’s no such sport as sport by sport o’erthrown,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: To make theirs ours and ours none but our own.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: So shall we stay, mocking intended game,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: And they, well mocked, depart away with shame.

speaker: BOYET
text: The trumpet sounds. Be masked; the maskers come.

speaker: BOY
text: All hail, the richest beauties on the Earth!

speaker: BOYET
text: Beauties no richer than rich taffeta.

speaker: BOY
text: A holy parcel of the fairest dames

speaker: BOY
text: That ever turned their—backs—to mortal views.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Their eyes, villain, their eyes!

speaker: BOY
text: That ever turned their eyes to mortal views.

speaker: BOY
text: Out—

speaker: BOYET
text: True; out indeed.

speaker: BOY
text: Out of your favors, heavenly spirits, vouchsafe

speaker: BOY
text: Not to behold—

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Once to behold, rogue!

speaker: BOY
text: Once to behold with your sun-beamèd eyes—

speaker: BOY
text: With your sun-beamèd eyes—

speaker: BOYET
text: They will not answer to that epithet.

speaker: BOYET
text: You were best call it “daughter-beamèd eyes.”

speaker: BOY
text: They do not mark me, and that brings me out.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Is this your perfectness? Begone, you rogue!

speaker: ROSALINE
text: What would these strangers? Know their minds, Boyet.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: If they do speak our language, ’tis our will

speaker: ROSALINE
text: That some plain man recount their purposes.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Know what they would.

speaker: BOYET
text: What would you with the Princess?

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Nothing but peace and gentle visitation.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: What would they, say they?

speaker: BOYET
text: Nothing but peace and gentle visitation.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Why, that they have, and bid them so be gone.

speaker: BOYET
text: She says you have it, and you may be gone.

speaker: KING
text: Say to her we have measured many miles

speaker: KING
text: To tread a measure with her on this grass.

speaker: BOYET
text: They say that they have measured many a mile

speaker: BOYET
text: To tread a measure with you on this grass.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: It is not so. Ask them how many inches

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Is in one mile. If they have measured many,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: The measure then of one is eas’ly told.

speaker: BOYET
text: If to come hither you have measured miles,

speaker: BOYET
text: And many miles, the Princess bids you tell

speaker: BOYET
text: How many inches doth fill up one mile.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Tell her we measure them by weary steps.

speaker: BOYET
text: She hears herself.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: How many weary steps

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Of many weary miles you have o’ergone

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Are numbered in the travel of one mile?

speaker: BEROWNE
text: We number nothing that we spend for you.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Our duty is so rich, so infinite,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: That we may do it still without account.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Vouchsafe to show the sunshine of your face

speaker: BEROWNE
text: That we, like savages, may worship it.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: My face is but a moon, and clouded too.

speaker: KING
text: Blessèd are clouds, to do as such clouds do!

speaker: KING
text: Vouchsafe, bright moon, and these thy stars, to shine,

speaker: KING
text: Those clouds removed, upon our watery eyne.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: O vain petitioner, beg a greater matter!

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Thou now requests but moonshine in the water.

speaker: KING
text: Then in our measure do but vouchsafe one change.

speaker: KING
text: Thou bidd’st me beg; this begging is not strange.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Play music, then. Nay, you must do it soon.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Not yet? No dance! Thus change I like the moon.

speaker: KING
text: Will you not dance? How come you thus estranged?

speaker: ROSALINE
text: You took the moon at full, but now she’s changed.

speaker: KING
text: Yet still she is the moon, and I the man.

speaker: KING
text: The music plays. Vouchsafe some motion to it.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Our ears vouchsafe it.

speaker: KING
text: But your legs should do it.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Since you are strangers and come here by chance,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: We’ll not be nice. Take hands. We will not dance.

speaker: KING
text: Why take we hands then?

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Only to part friends.—

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Curtsy, sweethearts—and so the measure ends.

speaker: KING
text: More measure of this measure! Be not nice.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: We can afford no more at such a price.

speaker: KING
text: Prize you yourselves. What buys your company?

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Your absence only.

speaker: KING
text: That can never be.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Then cannot we be bought. And so adieu—

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Twice to your visor, and half once to you.

speaker: KING
text: If you deny to dance, let’s hold more chat.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: In private, then.

speaker: KING
text: I am best pleased with that.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: White-handed mistress, one sweet word with thee.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Honey, and milk, and sugar—there is three.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Nay then, two treys, an if you grow so nice,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Metheglin, wort, and malmsey. Well run, dice!

speaker: BEROWNE
text: There’s half a dozen sweets.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Seventh sweet, adieu.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Since you can cog, I’ll play no more with you.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: One word in secret.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Let it not be sweet.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Thou grievest my gall.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Gall! Bitter.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Therefore meet.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: Will you vouchsafe with me to change a word?

speaker: MARIA
text: Name it.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: Fair lady—

speaker: MARIA
text: Say you so? Fair lord!

speaker: MARIA
text: Take that for your “fair lady.”

speaker: DUMAINE
text: Please it you

speaker: DUMAINE
text: As much in private, and I’ll bid adieu.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: What, was your vizard made without a tongue?

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: I know the reason, lady, why you ask.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: O, for your reason! Quickly, sir, I long.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: You have a double tongue within your mask,

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: And would afford my speechless vizard half.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Veal, quoth the Dutchman. Is not veal a calf?

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: A calf, fair lady?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: No, a fair Lord Calf.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: Let’s part the word.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: No, I’ll not be your half.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Take all and wean it. It may prove an ox.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: Look how you butt yourself in these sharp mocks.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: Will you give horns, chaste lady? Do not so.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Then die a calf before your horns do grow.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: One word in private with you ere I die.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Bleat softly, then. The butcher hears you cry.

speaker: BOYET
text: The tongues of mocking wenches are as keen

speaker: BOYET
text: As is the razor’s edge invisible,

speaker: BOYET
text: Cutting a smaller hair than may be seen;

speaker: BOYET
text: Above the sense of sense, so sensible

speaker: BOYET
text: Seemeth their conference. Their conceits have wings

speaker: BOYET
text: Fleeter than arrows, bullets, wind, thought, swifter things.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Not one word more, my maids. Break off, break off!

speaker: BEROWNE
text: By heaven, all dry-beaten with pure scoff!

speaker: KING
text: Farewell, mad wenches. You have simple wits.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Twenty adieus, my frozen Muskovits.—

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Are these the breed of wits so wondered at?

speaker: BOYET
text: Tapers they are, with your sweet breaths puffed out.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Well-liking wits they have; gross, gross; fat, fat.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: O poverty in wit, kingly-poor flout!

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Will they not, think you, hang themselves tonight?

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Or ever but in vizards show their faces?

speaker: PRINCESS
text: This pert Berowne was out of count’nance quite.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: They were all in lamentable cases.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: The King was weeping ripe for a good word.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Berowne did swear himself out of all suit.

speaker: MARIA
text: Dumaine was at my service, and his sword.

speaker: MARIA
text: “No point,” quoth I. My servant straight was mute.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Lord Longaville said I came o’er his heart.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And trow you what he called me?

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Qualm, perhaps.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Yes, in good faith.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Go, sickness as thou art!

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Well, better wits have worn plain statute-caps.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: But will you hear? The King is my love sworn.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: And quick Berowne hath plighted faith to me.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And Longaville was for my service born.

speaker: MARIA
text: Dumaine is mine as sure as bark on tree.

speaker: BOYET
text: Madam, and pretty mistresses, give ear.

speaker: BOYET
text: Immediately they will again be here

speaker: BOYET
text: In their own shapes, for it can never be

speaker: BOYET
text: They will digest this harsh indignity.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Will they return?

speaker: BOYET
text: They will, they will, God knows,

speaker: BOYET
text: And leap for joy, though they are lame with blows.

speaker: BOYET
text: Therefore change favors, and when they repair,

speaker: BOYET
text: Blow like sweet roses in this summer air.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: How “blow”? How “blow”? Speak to be understood.

speaker: BOYET
text: Fair ladies masked are roses in their bud.

speaker: BOYET
text: Dismasked, their damask sweet commixture shown,

speaker: BOYET
text: Are angels vailing clouds, or roses blown.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Avaunt, perplexity!—What shall we do

speaker: PRINCESS
text: If they return in their own shapes to woo?

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Good madam, if by me you’ll be advised,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Let’s mock them still, as well known as disguised.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Let us complain to them what fools were here,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Disguised like Muscovites in shapeless gear,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: And wonder what they were, and to what end

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Their shallow shows and prologue vilely penned,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: And their rough carriage so ridiculous,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Should be presented at our tent to us.

speaker: BOYET
text: Ladies, withdraw. The gallants are at hand.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Whip to our tents, as roes runs o’er land.

speaker: KING
text: Fair sir, God save you. Where’s the Princess?

speaker: BOYET
text: Gone to her tent. Please it your Majesty

speaker: BOYET
text: Command me any service to her thither?

speaker: KING
text: That she vouchsafe me audience for one word.

speaker: BOYET
text: I will, and so will she, I know, my lord.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: This fellow pecks up wit as pigeons peas,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: And utters it again when God doth please.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: He is wit’s peddler, and retails his wares

speaker: BEROWNE
text: At wakes and wassails, meetings, markets, fairs.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: And we that sell by gross, the Lord doth know,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Have not the grace to grace it with such show.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: This gallant pins the wenches on his sleeve.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Had he been Adam, he had tempted Eve.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: He can carve too, and lisp. Why, this is he

speaker: BEROWNE
text: That kissed his hand away in courtesy.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: This is the ape of form, Monsieur the Nice,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: That, when he plays at tables, chides the dice

speaker: BEROWNE
text: In honorable terms. Nay, he can sing

speaker: BEROWNE
text: A mean most meanly; and in ushering

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Mend him who can. The ladies call him sweet.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: The stairs, as he treads on them, kiss his feet.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: This is the flower that smiles on everyone

speaker: BEROWNE
text: To show his teeth as white as whale’s bone;

speaker: BEROWNE
text: And consciences that will not die in debt

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Pay him the due of “honey-tongued Boyet.”

speaker: KING
text: A blister on his sweet tongue, with my heart,

speaker: KING
text: That put Armado’s page out of his part!

speaker: BEROWNE
text: See where it comes! Behavior, what wert thou

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Till this madman showed thee? And what art thou now?

speaker: KING
text: All hail, sweet madam, and fair time of day.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: “Fair” in “all hail” is foul, as I conceive.

speaker: KING
text: Construe my speeches better, if you may.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Then wish me better. I will give you leave.

speaker: KING
text: We came to visit you, and purpose now

speaker: KING
text: To lead you to our court. Vouchsafe it, then.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: This field shall hold me, and so hold your vow.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Nor God nor I delights in perjured men.

speaker: KING
text: Rebuke me not for that which you provoke.

speaker: KING
text: The virtue of your eye must break my oath.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: You nickname virtue; “vice” you should have spoke,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: For virtue’s office never breaks men’s troth.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Now by my maiden honor, yet as pure

speaker: PRINCESS
text: As the unsullied lily, I protest,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: A world of torments though I should endure,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: I would not yield to be your house’s guest,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: So much I hate a breaking cause to be

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Of heavenly oaths vowed with integrity.

speaker: KING
text: O, you have lived in desolation here,

speaker: KING
text: Unseen, unvisited, much to our shame.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Not so, my lord. It is not so, I swear.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: We have had pastimes here and pleasant game.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: A mess of Russians left us but of late.

speaker: KING
text: How, madam? Russians?

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Ay, in truth, my lord.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Trim gallants, full of courtship and of state.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Madam, speak true.—It is not so, my lord.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: My lady, to the manner of the days,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: In courtesy gives undeserving praise.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: We four indeed confronted were with four

speaker: ROSALINE
text: In Russian habit. Here they stayed an hour

speaker: ROSALINE
text: And talked apace; and in that hour, my lord,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: They did not bless us with one happy word.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: I dare not call them fools; but this I think:

speaker: ROSALINE
text: When they are thirsty, fools would fain have drink.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: This jest is dry to me. Gentle sweet,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Your wits makes wise things foolish. When we greet,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: With eyes’ best seeing, heaven’s fiery eye,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: By light we lose light. Your capacity

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Is of that nature that to your huge store

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Wise things seem foolish and rich things but poor.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: This proves you wise and rich, for in my eye—

speaker: BEROWNE
text: I am a fool, and full of poverty.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: But that you take what doth to you belong,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: It were a fault to snatch words from my tongue.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: O, I am yours, and all that I possess!

speaker: ROSALINE
text: All the fool mine?

speaker: BEROWNE
text: I cannot give you less.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Which of the vizards was it that you wore?

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Where? When? What vizard? Why demand you this?

speaker: ROSALINE
text: There; then; that vizard; that superfluous case

speaker: ROSALINE
text: That hid the worse and showed the better face.

speaker: KING
text: We were descried. They’ll mock us now downright.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: Let us confess and turn it to a jest.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Amazed, my lord? Why looks your Highness sad?

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Help, hold his brows! He’ll swoon!—Why look you pale?

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Seasick, I think, coming from Muscovy.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Thus pour the stars down plagues for perjury.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Can any face of brass hold longer out?

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Here stand I, lady. Dart thy skill at me.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Bruise me with scorn, confound me with a flout.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Thrust thy sharp wit quite through my ignorance.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Cut me to pieces with thy keen conceit,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: And I will wish thee nevermore to dance,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Nor nevermore in Russian habit wait.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: O, never will I trust to speeches penned,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Nor to the motion of a schoolboy’s tongue,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Nor never come in vizard to my friend,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Nor woo in rhyme like a blind harper’s song.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Taffeta phrases, silken terms precise,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Three-piled hyperboles, spruce affectation,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Figures pedantical—these summer flies

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Have blown me full of maggot ostentation.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: I do forswear them, and I here protest

speaker: BEROWNE
text: By this white glove—how white the hand, God knows!—

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Henceforth my wooing mind shall be expressed

speaker: BEROWNE
text: In russet yeas and honest kersey noes.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: And to begin: Wench, so God help me, law,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: My love to thee is sound, sans crack or flaw.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Sans “sans,” I pray you.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Yet I have a trick

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Of the old rage. Bear with me, I am sick;

speaker: BEROWNE
text: I’ll leave it by degrees. Soft, let us see:

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Write “Lord have mercy on us” on those three.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: They are infected; in their hearts it lies.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: They have the plague, and caught it of your eyes.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: These lords are visited. You are not free,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: For the Lord’s tokens on you do I see.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: No, they are free that gave these tokens to us.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Our states are forfeit. Seek not to undo us.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: It is not so, for how can this be true,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: That you stand forfeit, being those that sue?

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Peace, for I will not have to do with you.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Nor shall not, if I do as I intend.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Speak for yourselves. My wit is at an end.

speaker: KING
text: Teach us, sweet madam, for our rude transgression

speaker: KING
text: Some fair excuse.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: The fairest is confession.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Were not you here but even now, disguised?

speaker: KING
text: Madam, I was.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: And were you well advised?

speaker: KING
text: I was, fair madam.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: When you then were here,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: What did you whisper in your lady’s ear?

speaker: KING
text: That more than all the world I did respect her.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: When she shall challenge this, you will reject her.

speaker: KING
text: Upon mine honor, no.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Peace, peace, forbear!

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Your oath once broke, you force not to forswear.

speaker: KING
text: Despise me when I break this oath of mine.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: I will, and therefore keep it.—Rosaline,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: What did the Russian whisper in your ear?

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Madam, he swore that he did hold me dear

speaker: ROSALINE
text: As precious eyesight, and did value me

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Above this world, adding thereto moreover

speaker: ROSALINE
text: That he would wed me or else die my lover.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: God give thee joy of him! The noble lord

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Most honorably doth uphold his word.

speaker: KING
text: What mean you, madam? By my life, my troth,

speaker: KING
text: I never swore this lady such an oath.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: By heaven, you did! And to confirm it plain,

speaker: ROSALINE
text:
You gave me this.
                   
                  She
                   
                  shows
                   
                  a
                   
                  token
                  .
                 But take it, sir, again.

speaker: KING
text: My faith and this the Princess I did give.

speaker: KING
text: I knew her by this jewel on her sleeve.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Pardon me, sir. This jewel did she wear.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: And Lord Berowne, I thank him, is my dear.

speaker: PRINCESS
text:
To
                 
                Berowne
                .
                 
              What, will you have me, or your pearl again?

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Neither of either. I remit both twain.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: I see the trick on ’t. Here was a consent,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Knowing aforehand of our merriment,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: To dash it like a Christmas comedy.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Some carry-tale, some please-man, some slight zany,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Some mumble-news, some trencher-knight, some Dick,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: That smiles his cheek in years and knows the trick

speaker: BEROWNE
text: To make my lady laugh when she’s disposed,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Told our intents before; which once disclosed,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: The ladies did change favors; and then we,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Following the signs, wooed but the sign of she.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Now, to our perjury to add more terror,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: We are again forsworn in will and error.

speaker: BEROWNE
text:
Much upon this ’tis.
                   
                  To
                   
                  Boyet
                  .
                 And might not you

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Forestall our sport, to make us thus untrue?

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Do not you know my lady’s foot by th’ squier?

speaker: BEROWNE
text: And laugh upon the apple of her eye?

speaker: BEROWNE
text: And stand between her back, sir, and the fire,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Holding a trencher, jesting merrily?

speaker: BEROWNE
text: You put our page out. Go, you are allowed.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Die when you will, a smock shall be your shroud.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: You leer upon me, do you? There’s an eye

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Wounds like a leaden sword.

speaker: BOYET
text: Full merrily

speaker: BOYET
text: Hath this brave manage, this career been run.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Lo, he is tilting straight! Peace, I have done.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Welcome, pure wit. Thou part’st a fair fray.

speaker: COSTARD
text: O Lord, sir, they would know

speaker: COSTARD
text: Whether the three Worthies shall come in or no.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: What, are there but three?

speaker: COSTARD
text: No, sir; but it is vara fine,

speaker: COSTARD
text: For every one pursents three.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: And three times thrice is nine.

speaker: COSTARD
text: Not so, sir, under correction, sir, I hope it is not so.

speaker: COSTARD
text: You cannot beg us, sir, I can assure you, sir; we know what we know.

speaker: COSTARD
text: I hope, sir, three times thrice, sir—

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Is not nine?

speaker: COSTARD
text: Under correction, sir, we know whereuntil it

speaker: COSTARD
text: doth amount.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: By Jove, I always took three threes for nine.

speaker: COSTARD
text: O Lord, sir, it were pity you should get your

speaker: COSTARD
text: living by reckoning, sir.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: How much is it?

speaker: COSTARD
text: O Lord, sir, the parties themselves, the actors,

speaker: COSTARD
text: sir, will show whereuntil it doth amount. For

speaker: COSTARD
text: mine own part, I am, as they say, but to parfect one

speaker: COSTARD
text: man in one poor man—Pompion the Great, sir.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Art thou one of the Worthies?

speaker: COSTARD
text: It pleased them to think me worthy of Pompey

speaker: COSTARD
text: the Great. For mine own part, I know not the

speaker: COSTARD
text: degree of the Worthy, but I am to stand for him.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Go bid them prepare.

speaker: COSTARD
text: We will turn it finely off, sir. We will take some care.

speaker: KING
text: Berowne, they will shame us. Let them not approach.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: We are shame-proof, my lord; and ’tis some policy

speaker: BEROWNE
text: To have one show worse than the King’s and his company.

speaker: KING
text: I say they shall not come.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Nay, my good lord, let me o’errule you now.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: That sport best pleases that doth least know how,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Where zeal strives to content, and the contents

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Dies in the zeal of that which it presents.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Their form confounded makes most form in mirth,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: When great things laboring perish in their birth.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: A right description of our sport, my lord.

speaker: ARMADO
text: Anointed, I implore so much expense

speaker: ARMADO
text: of thy royal sweet breath as will utter a brace

speaker: ARMADO
text: of words.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Doth this man serve God?

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Why ask you?

speaker: PRINCESS
text: He speaks not like a man of God his making.

speaker: ARMADO
text: That is all one, my fair sweet honey

speaker: ARMADO
text: monarch, for, I protest, the schoolmaster is exceeding

speaker: ARMADO
text: fantastical, too, too vain, too, too vain. But

speaker: ARMADO
text: we will put it, as they say, to fortuna de la guerra.—I

speaker: ARMADO
text: wish you the peace of mind, most royal

speaker: ARMADO
text: couplement!

speaker: KING
text: Here is like to be a good

speaker: KING
text: presence of Worthies. He presents Hector of Troy,

speaker: KING
text: the swain Pompey the Great, the parish curate

speaker: KING
text: Alexander, Armado’s page Hercules, the pedant

speaker: KING
text: Judas Maccabaeus.

speaker: KING
text: And if these four Worthies in their first show thrive,

speaker: KING
text: These four will change habits and present the other five.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: There is five in the first show.

speaker: KING
text: You are deceived. ’Tis not so.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: The pedant, the braggart, the hedge

speaker: BEROWNE
text: priest, the fool, and the boy.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Abate throw at novum, and the whole world again

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Cannot pick out five such, take each one in his vein.

speaker: KING
text: The ship is under sail, and here she comes amain.

speaker: COSTARD
text: I Pompey am—

speaker: BEROWNE
text: You lie; you are not he.

speaker: COSTARD
text: I Pompey am—

speaker: BOYET
text: With leopard’s head on knee.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Well said, old mocker. I must needs be friends with thee.

speaker: COSTARD
text: I Pompey am, Pompey, surnamed the Big—

speaker: DUMAINE
text: “The Great.”

speaker: COSTARD
text: It is “Great,” sir.—Pompey, surnamed the Great,

speaker: COSTARD
text: That oft in field, with targe and shield, did make my foe to sweat.

speaker: COSTARD
text: And traveling along this coast, I here am come by chance,

speaker: COSTARD
text: And lay my arms before the legs of this sweet lass of France.

speaker: COSTARD
text: If your Ladyship would say “Thanks, Pompey,” I

speaker: COSTARD
text: had done.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Great thanks, great Pompey.

speaker: COSTARD
text: ’Tis not so much worth, but I hope I was

speaker: COSTARD
text: perfect. I made a little fault in “Great.”

speaker: BEROWNE
text: My hat to a halfpenny, Pompey proves the

speaker: BEROWNE
text: best Worthy.

speaker: NATHANIEL
text: When in the world I lived, I was the world’s commander.

speaker: NATHANIEL
text: By east, west, north, and south, I spread my conquering might.

speaker: NATHANIEL
text: My scutcheon plain declares that I am Alisander—

speaker: BOYET
text: Your nose says no, you are not, for it stands too right.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Your nose smells “no” in this, most tender-smelling knight.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: The conqueror is dismayed.—Proceed, good Alexander.

speaker: NATHANIEL
text: When in the world I lived, I was the world’s commander—

speaker: BOYET
text: Most true; ’tis right. You were so, Alisander.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Pompey the Great—

speaker: COSTARD
text: Your servant, and Costard.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Take away the conqueror. Take away

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Alisander.

speaker: COSTARD
text: O sir, you have overthrown

speaker: COSTARD
text: Alisander the Conqueror. You will be scraped out of

speaker: COSTARD
text: the painted cloth for this. Your lion, that holds his

speaker: COSTARD
text: polax sitting on a close-stool, will be given to Ajax.

speaker: COSTARD
text: He will be the ninth Worthy. A conqueror, and

speaker: COSTARD
text: afeard to speak? Run away for shame, Alisander.

speaker: COSTARD
text: There, an ’t shall please you, a foolish mild man, an

speaker: COSTARD
text: honest man, look you, and soon dashed. He is a

speaker: COSTARD
text: marvelous good neighbor, faith, and a very good

speaker: COSTARD
text: bowler. But, for Alisander—alas, you see how ’tis—

speaker: COSTARD
text: a little o’erparted. But there are Worthies a-coming

speaker: COSTARD
text: will speak their mind in some other sort.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Stand aside, good Pompey.

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text: Great Hercules is presented by this imp,

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text: Whose club killed Cerberus, that three-headed canus,

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text: And when he was a babe, a child, a shrimp,

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text: Thus did he strangle serpents in his manus.

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text: Quoniam he seemeth in minority,

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text: Ergo I come with this apology.

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text:
To
                 
                Boy
                .
                 
              Keep some state in thy exit, and vanish.

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text: Judas I am—

speaker: DUMAINE
text: A Judas!

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text: Not Iscariot, sir.

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text: Judas I am, yclept Maccabaeus.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: Judas Maccabaeus clipped is plain Judas.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: A kissing traitor.—How art thou proved

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Judas?

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text: Judas I am—

speaker: DUMAINE
text: The more shame for you, Judas.

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text: What mean you, sir?

speaker: BOYET
text: To make Judas hang himself.

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text: Begin, sir, you are my elder.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Well followed. Judas was hanged on an

speaker: BEROWNE
text: elder.

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text: I will not be put out of countenance.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Because thou hast no face.

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text: What is this?

speaker: BOYET
text: A cittern-head.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: The head of a bodkin.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: A death’s face in a ring.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: The face of an old Roman coin, scarce

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: seen.

speaker: BOYET
text: The pommel of Caesar’s falchion.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: The carved-bone face on a flask.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Saint George’s half-cheek in a brooch.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: Ay, and in a brooch of lead.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Ay, and worn in the cap of a tooth-drawer.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: And now forward, for we have put thee in

speaker: BEROWNE
text: countenance.

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text: You have put me out of countenance.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: False. We have given thee faces.

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text: But you have outfaced them all.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: An thou wert a lion, we would do so.

speaker: BOYET
text: Therefore, as he is an ass, let him go.—

speaker: BOYET
text: And so adieu, sweet Jude. Nay, why dost thou stay?

speaker: DUMAINE
text: For the latter end of his name.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: For the “ass” to the “Jude”? Give it him.—Jud-as, away!

speaker: HOLOFERNES
text: This is not generous, not gentle, not humble.

speaker: BOYET
text: A light for Monsieur Judas! It grows dark; he may stumble.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Alas, poor Maccabaeus, how hath he been baited!

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Hide thy head, Achilles. Here comes Hector

speaker: BEROWNE
text: in arms.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: Though my mocks come home by me, I will

speaker: DUMAINE
text: now be merry.

speaker: KING
text: Hector was but a Troyan in respect of this.

speaker: BOYET
text: But is this Hector?

speaker: KING
text: I think Hector was not so clean-timbered.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: His leg is too big for Hector’s.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: More calf, certain.

speaker: BOYET
text: No, he is best endued in the small.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: This cannot be Hector.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: He’s a god or a painter, for he makes faces.

speaker: ARMADO
text: The armipotent Mars, of lances the almighty,

speaker: ARMADO
text: Gave Hector a gift—

speaker: DUMAINE
text: A gilt nutmeg.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: A lemon.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: Stuck with cloves.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: No, cloven.

speaker: ARMADO
text: Peace!

speaker: ARMADO
text: The armipotent Mars, of lances the almighty,

speaker: ARMADO
text: Gave Hector a gift, the heir of Ilion,

speaker: ARMADO
text: A man so breathed, that certain he would fight, yea,

speaker: ARMADO
text: From morn till night, out of his pavilion.

speaker: ARMADO
text: I am that flower—

speaker: DUMAINE
text: That mint.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: That columbine.

speaker: ARMADO
text: Sweet Lord Longaville, rein thy tongue.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: I must rather give it the rein, for it runs

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: against Hector.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: Ay, and Hector’s a greyhound.

speaker: ARMADO
text: The sweet warman is dead and rotten. Sweet

speaker: ARMADO
text: chucks, beat not the bones of the buried. When he

speaker: ARMADO
text: breathed, he was a man. But I will forward with my

speaker: ARMADO
text:
device.
                 
                To
                 
                Princess
                .
               Sweet royalty, bestow on me

speaker: ARMADO
text: the sense of hearing.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Speak, brave Hector. We are much delighted.

speaker: ARMADO
text: I do adore thy sweet Grace’s slipper.

speaker: BOYET
text: Loves her by the foot.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: He may not by the yard.

speaker: ARMADO
text: This Hector far surmounted Hannibal.

speaker: ARMADO
text: The party is gone—

speaker: COSTARD
text: Fellow Hector, she is gone; she is two

speaker: COSTARD
text: months on her way.

speaker: ARMADO
text: What meanest thou?

speaker: COSTARD
text: Faith, unless you play the honest Troyan, the

speaker: COSTARD
text: poor wench is cast away. She’s quick; the child

speaker: COSTARD
text: brags in her belly already. ’Tis yours.

speaker: ARMADO
text: Dost thou infamonize me among potentates?

speaker: ARMADO
text: Thou shalt die!

speaker: COSTARD
text: Then shall Hector be whipped for Jaquenetta,

speaker: COSTARD
text: that is quick by him, and hanged for Pompey,

speaker: COSTARD
text: that is dead by him.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: Most rare Pompey!

speaker: BOYET
text: Renowned Pompey!

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Greater than “Great”! Great, great, great

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Pompey. Pompey the Huge!

speaker: DUMAINE
text: Hector trembles.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Pompey is moved. More Ates, more Ates!

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Stir them on, stir them on.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: Hector will challenge him.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Ay, if he have no more man’s blood in his

speaker: BEROWNE
text: belly than will sup a flea.

speaker: ARMADO
text: By the North Pole, I do challenge

speaker: ARMADO
text: thee!

speaker: COSTARD
text: I will not fight with a pole like a northern

speaker: COSTARD
text: man! I’ll slash. I’ll do it by the sword.—I bepray

speaker: COSTARD
text: you, let me borrow my arms again.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: Room for the incensed Worthies!

speaker: COSTARD
text: I’ll do it in my shirt.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: Most resolute Pompey!

speaker: BOY
text: Master, let me take you a buttonhole

speaker: BOY
text: lower. Do you not see Pompey is uncasing for the

speaker: BOY
text: combat? What mean you? You will lose your

speaker: BOY
text: reputation.

speaker: ARMADO
text: Gentlemen and soldiers, pardon me. I will

speaker: ARMADO
text: not combat in my shirt.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: You may not deny it. Pompey hath made the

speaker: DUMAINE
text: challenge.

speaker: ARMADO
text: Sweet bloods, I both may and will.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: What reason have you for ’t?

speaker: ARMADO
text: The naked truth of it is, I have no shirt. I go

speaker: ARMADO
text: woolward for penance.

speaker: BOYET
text: True, and it was enjoined him in Rome for want

speaker: BOYET
text: of linen; since when, I’ll be sworn, he wore none

speaker: BOYET
text: but a dishclout of Jaquenetta’s, and that he wears

speaker: BOYET
text: next his heart for a favor.

speaker: MARCADE
text: God save you, madam.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Welcome, Marcade,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: But that thou interruptest our merriment.

speaker: MARCADE
text: I am sorry, madam, for the news I bring

speaker: MARCADE
text: Is heavy in my tongue. The King your father—

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Dead, for my life.

speaker: MARCADE
text: Even so. My tale is told.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Worthies, away! The scene begins to cloud.

speaker: ARMADO
text: For mine own part, I breathe free breath. I

speaker: ARMADO
text: have seen the day of wrong through the little hole

speaker: ARMADO
text: of discretion, and I will right myself like a soldier.

speaker: KING
text: How fares your Majesty?

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Boyet, prepare. I will away tonight.

speaker: KING
text: Madam, not so. I do beseech you stay.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Prepare, I say.—I thank you, gracious lords,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: For all your fair endeavors, and entreat,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Out of a new-sad soul, that you vouchsafe

speaker: PRINCESS
text: In your rich wisdom to excuse or hide

speaker: PRINCESS
text: The liberal opposition of our spirits,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: If overboldly we have borne ourselves

speaker: PRINCESS
text: In the converse of breath; your gentleness

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Was guilty of it. Farewell, worthy lord.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: A heavy heart bears not a humble tongue.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Excuse me so, coming too short of thanks

speaker: PRINCESS
text: For my great suit so easily obtained.

speaker: KING
text: The extreme parts of time extremely forms

speaker: KING
text: All causes to the purpose of his speed,

speaker: KING
text: And often at his very loose decides

speaker: KING
text: That which long process could not arbitrate.

speaker: KING
text: And though the mourning brow of progeny

speaker: KING
text: Forbid the smiling courtesy of love

speaker: KING
text: The holy suit which fain it would convince,

speaker: KING
text: Yet since love’s argument was first on foot,

speaker: KING
text: Let not the cloud of sorrow jostle it

speaker: KING
text: From what it purposed, since to wail friends lost

speaker: KING
text: Is not by much so wholesome-profitable

speaker: KING
text: As to rejoice at friends but newly found.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: I understand you not. My griefs are double.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Honest plain words best pierce the ear of grief,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: And by these badges understand the King:

speaker: BEROWNE
text: For your fair sakes have we neglected time,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Played foul play with our oaths. Your beauty, ladies,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Hath much deformed us, fashioning our humors

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Even to the opposèd end of our intents.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: And what in us hath seemed ridiculous—

speaker: BEROWNE
text: As love is full of unbefitting strains,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: All wanton as a child, skipping and vain,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Formed by the eye and therefore, like the eye,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Full of strange shapes, of habits, and of forms,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Varying in subjects as the eye doth roll

speaker: BEROWNE
text: To every varied object in his glance;

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Which parti-coated presence of loose love

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Put on by us, if, in your heavenly eyes,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Have misbecomed our oaths and gravities,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Those heavenly eyes, that look into these faults,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Suggested us to make. Therefore, ladies,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Our love being yours, the error that love makes

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Is likewise yours. We to ourselves prove false

speaker: BEROWNE
text: By being once false forever to be true

speaker: BEROWNE
text: To those that make us both—fair ladies, you.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: And even that falsehood, in itself a sin,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Thus purifies itself and turns to grace.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: We have received your letters full of love;

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Your favors, the ambassadors of love;

speaker: PRINCESS
text: And in our maiden council rated them

speaker: PRINCESS
text: At courtship, pleasant jest, and courtesy,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: As bombast and as lining to the time.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: But more devout than this in our respects

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Have we not been, and therefore met your loves

speaker: PRINCESS
text: In their own fashion, like a merriment.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: Our letters, madam, showed much more than jest.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: So did our looks.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: We did not quote them so.

speaker: KING
text: Now, at the latest minute of the hour,

speaker: KING
text: Grant us your loves.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: A time, methinks, too short

speaker: PRINCESS
text: To make a world-without-end bargain in.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: No, no, my lord, your Grace is perjured much,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Full of dear guiltiness, and therefore this:

speaker: PRINCESS
text: If for my love—as there is no such cause—

speaker: PRINCESS
text: You will do aught, this shall you do for me:

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Your oath I will not trust, but go with speed

speaker: PRINCESS
text: To some forlorn and naked hermitage,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Remote from all the pleasures of the world.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: There stay until the twelve celestial signs

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Have brought about the annual reckoning.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: If this austere insociable life

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Change not your offer made in heat of blood;

speaker: PRINCESS
text: If frosts and fasts, hard lodging, and thin weeds

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Nip not the gaudy blossoms of your love,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: But that it bear this trial, and last love;

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Then, at the expiration of the year,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Come challenge me, challenge me by these deserts,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: And by this virgin palm now kissing thine,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: I will be thine. And till that instant shut

speaker: PRINCESS
text: My woeful self up in a mourning house,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Raining the tears of lamentation

speaker: PRINCESS
text: For the remembrance of my father’s death.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: If this thou do deny, let our hands part,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Neither entitled in the other’s heart.

speaker: KING
text: If this, or more than this, I would deny,

speaker: KING
text: To flatter up these powers of mine with rest,

speaker: KING
text: The sudden hand of death close up mine eye!

speaker: KING
text: Hence hermit, then. My heart is in thy breast.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: But what to me, my love? But what to me?

speaker: DUMAINE
text: A wife?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: A beard, fair health, and honesty.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: With threefold love I wish you all these three.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: O, shall I say “I thank you, gentle wife”?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Not so, my lord. A twelvemonth and a day

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I’ll mark no words that smooth-faced wooers say.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Come when the King doth to my lady come;

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Then, if I have much love, I’ll give you some.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: I’ll serve thee true and faithfully till then.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Yet swear not, lest you be forsworn again.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: What says Maria?

speaker: MARIA
text: At the twelvemonth’s end

speaker: MARIA
text: I’ll change my black gown for a faithful friend.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: I’ll stay with patience, but the time is long.

speaker: MARIA
text: The liker you; few taller are so young.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Studies my lady? Mistress, look on me.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Behold the window of my heart, mine eye,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: What humble suit attends thy answer there.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Impose some service on me for thy love.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Oft have I heard of you, my Lord Berowne,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Before I saw you; and the world’s large tongue

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Proclaims you for a man replete with mocks,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Full of comparisons and wounding flouts,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Which you on all estates will execute

speaker: ROSALINE
text: That lie within the mercy of your wit.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: To weed this wormwood from your fruitful brain,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: And therewithal to win me, if you please,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Without the which I am not to be won,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: You shall this twelvemonth term from day to day

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Visit the speechless sick, and still converse

speaker: ROSALINE
text: With groaning wretches; and your task shall be,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: With all the fierce endeavor of your wit,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: To enforce the painèd impotent to smile.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: To move wild laughter in the throat of death?

speaker: BEROWNE
text: It cannot be, it is impossible.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Mirth cannot move a soul in agony.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Why, that’s the way to choke a gibing spirit,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Whose influence is begot of that loose grace

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Which shallow laughing hearers give to fools.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: A jest’s prosperity lies in the ear

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Of him that hears it, never in the tongue

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Of him that makes it. Then if sickly ears,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Deafed with the clamors of their own dear groans

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Will hear your idle scorns, continue then,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: And I will have you and that fault withal.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: But if they will not, throw away that spirit,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: And I shall find you empty of that fault,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Right joyful of your reformation.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: A twelvemonth? Well, befall what will befall,

speaker: BEROWNE
text: I’ll jest a twelvemonth in an hospital.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Ay, sweet my lord, and so I take my leave.

speaker: KING
text: No, madam, we will bring you on your way.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Our wooing doth not end like an old play.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Jack hath not Jill. These ladies’ courtesy

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Might well have made our sport a comedy.

speaker: KING
text: Come, sir, it wants a twelvemonth and a day,

speaker: KING
text: And then ’twill end.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: That’s too long for a play.

speaker: ARMADO
text: Sweet Majesty, vouchsafe me—

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Was not that Hector?

speaker: DUMAINE
text: The worthy knight of Troy.

speaker: ARMADO
text: I will kiss thy royal finger, and take leave. I

speaker: ARMADO
text: am a votary; I have vowed to Jaquenetta to hold the

speaker: ARMADO
text: plow for her sweet love three year. But, most

speaker: ARMADO
text: esteemed Greatness, will you hear the dialogue that

speaker: ARMADO
text: the two learned men have compiled in praise of the

speaker: ARMADO
text: owl and the cuckoo? It should have followed in the

speaker: ARMADO
text: end of our show.

speaker: KING
text: Call them forth quickly. We will do so.

speaker: ARMADO
text: Holla! Approach.

speaker: ARMADO
text: This side is Hiems, Winter; this Ver, the Spring; the

speaker: ARMADO
text: one maintained by the owl, th’ other by the cuckoo.

speaker: ARMADO
text: Ver, begin.

speaker: SPRING
text: When daisies pied and violets blue,

speaker: SPRING
text: And lady-smocks all silver-white,

speaker: SPRING
text: And cuckoo-buds of yellow hue

speaker: SPRING
text: Do paint the meadows with delight,

speaker: SPRING
text: The cuckoo then on every tree

speaker: SPRING
text: Mocks married men; for thus sings he:

speaker: SPRING
text: “Cuckoo!

speaker: SPRING
text: Cuckoo, cuckoo!” O word of fear,

speaker: SPRING
text: Unpleasing to a married ear.

speaker: SPRING
text: When shepherds pipe on oaten straws,

speaker: SPRING
text: And merry larks are plowmen’s clocks;

speaker: SPRING
text: When turtles tread, and rooks and daws,

speaker: SPRING
text: And maidens bleach their summer smocks;

speaker: SPRING
text: The cuckoo then on every tree

speaker: SPRING
text: Mocks married men, for thus sings he:

speaker: SPRING
text: “Cuckoo!

speaker: SPRING
text: Cuckoo, cuckoo!” O word of fear,

speaker: SPRING
text: Unpleasing to a married ear.

speaker: WINTER
text: When icicles hang by the wall,

speaker: WINTER
text: And Dick the shepherd blows his nail,

speaker: WINTER
text: And Tom bears logs into the hall,

speaker: WINTER
text: And milk comes frozen home in pail;

speaker: WINTER
text: When blood is nipped, and ways be foul,

speaker: WINTER
text: Then nightly sings the staring owl

speaker: WINTER
text: “Tu-whit to-who.” A merry note,

speaker: WINTER
text: While greasy Joan doth keel the pot.

speaker: WINTER
text: When all aloud the wind doth blow,

speaker: WINTER
text: And coughing drowns the parson’s saw,

speaker: WINTER
text: And birds sit brooding in the snow,

speaker: WINTER
text: And Marian’s nose looks red and raw;

speaker: WINTER
text: When roasted crabs hiss in the bowl,

speaker: WINTER
text: Then nightly sings the staring owl

speaker: WINTER
text: “Tu-whit to-who.” A merry note,

speaker: WINTER
text: While greasy Joan doth keel the pot.

speaker: ARMADO
text: The words of Mercury are harsh after the

speaker: ARMADO
text: songs of Apollo. You that way; we this way.
